channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What to know as Israel expands its operations in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes hammered targets across southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs while its military urged residents to flee more than two dozen Lebanese towns and villages, further signs that Israel is ramping up its operations in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel extends evacuation warnings in Lebanon, signaling a wider offensive
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military on Thursday warned people to evacuate a city and other communities in southern Lebanon that are north of a U.N.-declared buffer zone, signaling that it may widen a ground operation launched earlier this week against the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US organizing flights out of Lebanon, offering loans for tickets
item.description: The U.S. is organizing flights out of Lebanon to help evacuate its citizens and offering loans for those who cannot purchase flight tickets. So far, around 250 Americans and their family members have departed Lebanon this week on two flights organized by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 2 teens detained following explosions near Denmark's Israeli Embassy
item.description: Two Swedish teenagers were detained Thursday on preliminary charges in connection with a pair of explosions near the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 78 people died when a boat with 278 on board capsized in eastern Congo, official says
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 78 people died when an overcrowded boat capsized on Lake Kivu in eastern Congo on Thursday, a local governor said. A frantic search and rescue operation was underway hours later as many remained unaccounted for from the vessel, believed to have had 278 people on board.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Justice Department takes action against Russian hacking group
item.description: U.S. officials have seized dozens of web domains used by Russian intelligence agents and their proxies to steal Americans’ information, the Justice Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: South Africa's top court will decide if impeachment proceedings against the president can be revived
item.description: South Africa’s Constitutional Court says it will hear a case next month brought by two opposition parties to revive impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukrainian stronghold Vuhledar falls to Russian offensive after two years of bombardment
item.description: Vuhledar fell to Russian troops this week after Ukrainian forces were forced to withdraw to preserve lives and military equipment after more than two years of bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum called “deplorable.”.
item.dc:creator: Mark Stevenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italian family believes painting found in a dump in 1960s is a Picasso and seeks authentication
item.description: An Italian family hopes to prove definitively that a painting discarded from a villa on Capri more than 60 years ago is a Picasso.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dozens of migrants still missing off Djibouti's coast after smugglers forced them out of boats
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — Rescuers searched Thursday for dozens of migrants from Africa still missing after smugglers apparently forced them out of two boats in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti earlier this week, the Djiboutian coast guard said.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Belarus opposition urges immediate release of over 200 political prisoners in dire state
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Belarusian opposition leader in exile Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya on Thursday said 224 political prisoners in Belarus must be freed urgently, and called on Western countries to negotiate their release with the Belarusian authorities.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US Navy shows destroyer firing interceptor at Iranian missile
item.description: The Department of Defense confirmed two U.S. naval ships intercepted and destroyed missiles launched by Iran on the eastern Mediterranean Sea on Tuesday. USS Bulkeley (DDG-84) and USS Cole (DDG-67) fired a “dozen interceptors” at missiles targeting Israel.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: In time for Oct. 7 anniversary, a new film documents Hamas' attack on Israel music festival
item.description: A new documentary captures the horror of the Hamas attack on a music festival in Israel, in time for the first anniversary of the event.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US bans steel, artificial sweetener imports from Chinese companies over forced labor allegations
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security announced Wednesday it will no longer import steel or artificial sweeteners from two companies based in China due to their alleged use of forced labor. The department said Baowu Group Xinjiang Bayi Iron and Steel Co. Ltd.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Palestinians’ approval of US leadership at record low: Gallup
item.description: The approval rating in Palestine of U.S. leaders has dropped to a record low, a new survey found. Just 5 percent of Palestinians in the West Bank and East Jerusalem approve of U.S. leadership, the lowest on record since 2006, according to a poll conducted by Gallup.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: UK gives sovereignty of the long-contested Chagos Islands to Mauritius. A key US base remains
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government agreed Thursday to hand sovereignty of the long-contested Chagos Islands, an archipelago of more than 60 islands in the Indian Ocean, to Mauritius, in a deal to secure the future of a strategically important U.K.-U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Victor Perahia, Holocaust survivor and president of French Union of Auschwitz Deportees, dies at 91
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Victor Perahia, the president of the French Union of Auschwitz Deportees and a Holocaust survivor, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dominican Republic will deport up to 10,000 Haitians a week, citing an 'excess' of immigrants
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The Dominican Republic announced Wednesday that it would start massive deportations of Haitians living illegally in the country, expelling up to 10,000 of them a week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Catholic reform debate launched by Pope Francis leans away from ordained roles for women
item.description: ROME (AP) — Debate was leaning away from allowing women to take on ordained roles in the Catholic Church as the second phase of Pope Francis’ reform project opened Wednesday with an agenda topped by calls for women to take up more positions of responsibility Francis presided over an opening Mass in
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese Communist Party marks 75 years of rule as economic, security threats persist
item.description: China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule. The state-controlled media reported on China's progress and stability, with no mention of the many challenges facing the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden opposes Israeli attack on Iranian nuclear sites
item.description: President Biden said Wednesday that he does not support Israel carrying out strikes on Iranian nuclear sites as the U.S. and its allies mull additional sanctions against Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Mexico's army seizes local police weapons in cartel heartland amid gunfights, violence
item.description: The Mexican army has seized the firearms of local police in the Sinaloa city of Culiacan as cartel violence has escalated, dozens have been killed in the streets and distrust in the police grows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will Netanyahu 'take the fight directly to Iran' after massive missile strike across Israel?
item.description: Amid the most lethal Iranian missile strike into Israel territory, military experts are urging Israel to take off the gloves and punish the mullahs in Iran with devastating warfare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis' approval of US leadership drops since Oct. 7 Hamas attack
item.description: The approval rating in Israel of U.S. leaders has dropped since the Oct. 7 Hamas attack, a new survey found. Sixty-three percent of Israelis approve of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 4 candidates to lead Britain's defeated Conservatives bash Labour, and each other
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, England (AP) — The four contenders to lead Britain’s Conservative Party took turns in the spotlight on Wednesday, each claiming to be the one who can lead the right-of-center party back from a catastrophic election defeat.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli troops battle Hezbollah in Lebanon as fears of a wider war mount after Iran's missile attack
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli troops battled Hezbollah militants inside Lebanon on Wednesday after launching what they say are limited ground operations there.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military says regular infantry, armored units joining limited ground operation in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said regular infantry and armored units were joining what it has called its limited and localized ground operations in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Iran attacks mean for Americans in Israel: Vittert
item.description: NewsNation's Leland Vittert believes there will not be immediate panic for most of the more than 500,000 Americans living in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old ancient community in Morocco
item.description: An ancient farming community was discovered in Morocco. Items found around the 5,000-year-old community included animal remains, pottery, stone, axes and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
